connect.title: Automatic monitoring
connect.subtitle: Connect {{name}}'s device to receive metrics in real time.
connect.compatNote: Compatible with Tasker (Android), Shortcuts (iOS), companion apps or any system that can POST. The daily cron will analyze the data automatically every day at 08:00.
connect.iosStep1: Open Shortcuts on the kid's iPhone → create a new shortcut.
connect.iosStep4: Schedule with Personal Automation → At a time (e.g. every night at 11pm). It will run on its own and send the data.
connect.csvHint: Family Link export, Screen Time export, or own format
connect.altHeaders: Alternative headers accepted: timestamp, app_name, duration_seconds, duration_minutes.
connect.rotateConfirm: This will invalidate the current token. You'll have to reconfigure your devices. Continue?

alerts.title: Alerts
alerts.subtitle: Emotional state + actionable advice. We refer to professionals when critical.
alerts.proTitle: Critical alerts detected. You're not alone.
alerts.proDesc: If you notice signs of distress (isolation, self-harm, severe cyberbullying, suicidal ideation), reach out to a professional now. These resources are free and confidential:
alerts.howToTalk: How to talk about it with your kid
alerts.needHelpDesc: If signs persist or worsen, don't wait. Call the resource below — talking to an expert can change everything.

adv.night1: Tonight, charge the phone OUTSIDE the bedroom.
adv.night2: Turn on grayscale mode from 9pm for one week.
adv.social1: Agree a realistic daily limit (30-45 min) using 'app_time_limit' in Rules.
adv.social2: Swap 10 min of scrolling for 10 min of offline activity (walk, sport, drawing).
adv.burst1: Configure a 'session_burst' rule to detect compulsive use automatically.
adv.forbidden1: Talk without punishing: ask what they were looking for in that app before setting a rule.
adv.generic1: Wait 1-2 hours and bring it up in a neutral moment, not heated.
adv.generic2: Ask how they feel, not how long they've been on the phone.
adv.generic3: Agree together on one small change (1 rule, not 10) and review in 7 days.

talk.preventive: I noticed something in the data and wanted to ask how you felt today with the phone. No anger, just curious.
talk.moderate: I want to understand what's going on this week. What does that app give you? How does it leave you after?
talk.critical: I'm here, no judgment. What matters is how YOU are. Want us to look for help together?

learn.title: Learning hub
learn.subtitle: Actionable micro-lessons for parents. Mark the ones you apply.

talk.title: How to talk to your kid about phone use
talk.body: Create a no-judgment space to talk about digital life without turning it into an interrogation.
steps: Pick a neutral moment (walk, car), never right after an alert.
Start by asking what they enjoy, not how long they spend.
Share one of your own bad habits first to build trust.
Agree together on one simple rule: screen-free zones or moments.
talk.tip: Avoid 'you're on your phone all day'. Replace with 'what does this app give you?'.

warning.title: Warning signs on social media
warning.body: Early patterns that may signal anxiety, toxic comparison or isolation.
steps: Watch for sudden mood changes right after phone use.
Monitor night use >30min and compulsive opens (>10 unlocks/hour).
Detect dependence on a single app (>70% of time).
Talk to your kid if 2+ signs appear within a week.
warning.tip: Set a 'session_burst' rule in Rules to detect it automatically.

night.title: Night digital hygiene
night.body: Sleep is the first thing affected by digital use. Restoring it changes everything.
steps: No screens 60 minutes before sleep.
Charge the phone OUTSIDE the bedroom (golden rule).
Enable grayscale mode from 9pm.
Create a 7-day quest in Quests: 'Sleep without phone'.
night.tip: Blue light delays melatonin up to 90 minutes. The body does not 'get used to it'.

habits.title: Building healthy digital habits
habits.body: Better to design the environment than to rely on the kid's willpower.
steps: Remove non-essential notifications from the kid's phone.
Move social apps to a secondary folder, off the home screen.
Set family moments with all phones in a box.
Reinforce healthy days positively with points in Quests.
habits.tip: Children's brains learn by reinforcement. Reward presence, not absence.

dopamine.title: Dopamine, infinite scroll and attention
dopamine.body: Why TikTok, Reels and Shorts are so addictive for developing brains.
steps: Explain 'variable reward' with an example (slot machine).
Agree a realistic daily limit for scroll apps (e.g. 30-45 min).
Activate 'app_time_limit' in Rules for that specific app.
Replace 10 min of scrolling with 10 min of offline activity each day.
dopamine.tip: The problem is not the app, it's the format. Long video > infinite scroll.

cyber.title: Cyberbullying: detect and act
cyber.body: Digital harassment leaves invisible emotional marks. Knowing how to act is essential.
steps: Create a confidential channel: 'you can tell me without punishment'.
If suspected, do NOT delete messages: take screenshots with visible date.
Report content on the platform itself (all have a button).
If serious or repeated, contact local authorities or helpline.
cyber.tip: A kid's silence is not indifference, it's fear. Ask without pressure.

selfimage.title: Self-image and filters: protect it
selfimage.body: Filters and constant comparison erode self-esteem in teens.
steps: Talk about how influencer photos are edited (show before/after).
Follow accounts together that show real bodies and lives.
Avoid commenting on your kid's appearance daily; value other things.
If you see signs (diets, avoiding photos, anxiety), seek professional help.
selfimage.tip: Studies show reducing 1h/day of social media improves self-esteem in 3 weeks.

boundaries.title: Setting limits without battles
boundaries.body: Limits are respected when understood, not when imposed.
steps: Explain the why (sleep, attention, real life), not just the what.
Use the app to show objective data: 'this week 18h on TikTok'.
Agree consequences together BEFORE, not during a conflict.
Recognize their progress with points in Quests when they comply.
boundaries.tip: Firm and warm beats harsh or absent. Aim for 'firm + warm'.

wizard.title: Connect {{name}}'s device
wizard.subtitle: Step-by-step assistant. In under 2 minutes you'll see live data flowing.
wizard.choosePlatform: Which device does your kid use?
wizard.rotateConfirm: This will invalidate the current token. Continue?
wizard.qrHelp: Open the iPhone/Android camera on the kid's phone and scan. URL and token ready to paste.
wizard.readyTitle: All set. Send a test event or wait for the first real signal.
wizard.waitingBody: Run the shortcut on the kid's device. We'll detect the connection automatically.
wizard.successBody: Metrics are flowing in real time. AI will start analyzing patterns.
wizard.csvHint: Family Link, Screen Time or custom format

ios.s1Title: Open the Shortcuts app on the kid's iPhone
ios.s1Body: It's the gray app with two colored shapes. Pre-installed on iOS. Tap + at the top right to create a new shortcut.
ios.s2Title: Scan the QR to set URL and token
ios.s2Body: Point the camera at the QR below. You'll get the URL and your kid's unique token, only used in this shortcut.
ios.s3Body: Method: POST · Header: Content-Type: application/json · Paste the JSON below as request body. Optional: add 'Get Screen Time' first (iOS 17+).
ios.s4Title: Schedule the shortcut and send the first signal
ios.s4Body: In Shortcuts → Automation → Time of Day, repeat every night at 23:00. Run the shortcut once now to send the first signal.

and.s1Title: Install Tasker or HTTP Shortcuts on the kid's Android
and.s1Body: Tasker (Play Store) or the free 'HTTP Request Shortcuts' app work great. Open it and create a new task/shortcut.
and.s2Title: Scan the QR to copy URL and token
and.s2Body: Point the Android camera at the QR. Paste the URL as POST target and the token in the JSON body.
and.s3Title: Set up the POST request with the JSON
and.s3Body: Method: POST · Header: Content-Type: application/json · Body: the JSON below. In Tasker, use the 'HTTP Request' action.
and.s4Body: In Tasker → Profiles → Time, set it daily at 23:00 (or whenever). Run once to send the first signal.

quick.title: Connect {{name}}'s phone in 60 seconds
quick.subtitle: Scan the QR with the kid's phone, run the test, done. Skip the manual setup.
quick.step1Title: Open the camera on your kid's phone
quick.step1Body: Point at the QR. iOS Shortcuts / Android HTTP Shortcuts will auto-fill URL and token.
quick.step2Title: Tap 'Send first event' (or use the test button)
quick.step2Body: We instantly detect the connection. No accounts, no spyware, just metadata.
quick.step3Body: Predictions, alerts and quests update in real time as data arrives.
quick.rotateConfirm: This will invalidate the current token. Continue?
